titleStmt.title: Tel Aviv, Tel Aviv University Sourasky Central Library, Faitlovich 7
Faitlovich 7: Prayer of the Virgin at Bartos, Four Falasha prayers, Book of the disciples (Arde'et), Acts of Moses

editionStmt.p: The initial version of the record provided with the data available from the Tel Aviv University Library

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msIdentifier.repository: Tel Aviv University Sourasky Central Library

msContents.summary: Fol. 1a: The prayer of the Virgin at Bartos (Beyrouth?), known from the manuscript CLXVIII. 4 in Wrights catalogue of Ethiopian manuscripts in the British Museum, page 112. Compare also Dillmann's catalogue of Berlin manuscripts no.73,2 and 74,1, and Zotenberg's catalogue of Paris manuscripts nos. 56, 57,1 etc. -- Fol. 33b: Four Falasha prayers: 33b - a prayer published by Aescoly in his recueil de textes Falachas page 152, according to a manuscript in the collection d'Abaddie -- 34b - a magic prayer -- 38a - A prayer for the Sabbath -- 39a - another magic prayer -- Fol. 40a: The book of the disciples (Arde'et). See remarks to manuscript no.6 -- Fol. 46a: The acts of Moses. This text is published according to a manuscript of the collection d'Abbadie by Aescoly in his above mentioned book, as a continuation of the book of the disciples. Our text reaches until page 143 of Aescoly's book

msItem.title: The prayer of the Virgin at Bartos
msItem.note: known from the manuscript CLXVIII. 4 in Wrights catalogue of Ethiopian manuscripts in the British Museum, page 112. Compare also Dillmann's catalogue of Berlin manuscripts no.73,2 and 74,1, and Zotenberg's catalogue of Paris manuscripts nos. 56, 57,1 etc.

msItem.title: Four Beta ʾƎsrāʾel prayers
Prayer 1
msItem.note: a prayer published by Aescoly in Recueil de textes falachas. Introduction. Textes éthiopiens. (édition critique et traduction). IndexTravaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologieAaron ZeevAešcolyParisInstitut d’ethnologie1951Travaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologie5555https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/bpt6k26168q.imageBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951RecueilBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951Recueil152, according to a manuscript in the collection d'Abaddie

msItem.title: Prayer 3: A prayer for the Sabbath

msItem.note: All these prayers are published by Aescoly Recueil de textes falachas. Introduction. Textes éthiopiens. (édition critique et traduction). IndexTravaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologieAaron ZeevAešcolyParisInstitut d’ethnologie1951Travaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologie5555https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/bpt6k26168q.imageBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951RecueilBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951Recueil, out of a manuscript in the collection d'Abbadie. 38a corresponds to Cantique Aescoly p. 152 -- 39b corresponds to Hymne Aescoly p. 156 -- 41a corresponds to Psaume et Prière Aescoly p. 158 -- 48b. corresponds to Prière magique Aescoly p. 176 -- 56a. corresponds to Prière magique Aescoly p. 186

msItem.title: The book of the disciples (Arde'et) (Beta ʾƎsrāʾel version)
msItem.note: The Beta ʾƎsrāʾel version has been published by Aescoly in Recueil de textes falachas. Introduction. Textes éthiopiens. (édition critique et traduction). IndexTravaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologieAaron ZeevAešcolyParisInstitut d’ethnologie1951Travaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologie5555https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/bpt6k26168q.imageBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951RecueilBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951Recueil, together with a French translation. His text is based on the manuscript of the collection d'Abbadie in the Bibliothèque Nationale of Paris. The text of the present manuscript shows numerous variant readings and complete certain lacunae in the text published by Aescoly. It includes the book designed as "Acts of Moses" (compare Wrights catalogue Ms CLXIX) and reaches until page 142 of Aescoly's printed text

msItem.title: The acts of Moses
msItem.note: This text is published according to a manuscript of the collection d'Abbadie by Aescoly in Recueil de textes falachas. Introduction. Textes éthiopiens. (édition critique et traduction). IndexTravaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologieAaron ZeevAešcolyParisInstitut d’ethnologie1951Travaux et mémoires de l’Institut d’ethnologie5555https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/bpt6k26168q.imageBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951RecueilBeta Esraelbm:Aescoly1951Recueil, as a continuation of the book of the disciples. Our text reaches until page 143 of Aescoly's book

supportDesc.extent:
64 130 95
4 blank leaves at the beginning and 3 at the end of the volume

handDesc.handNote: Nice, careful writing. The manuscript consists of two parts. Fol. 1-33a is distinctly Christian. With fol. 33b starts the Falasha section of the manuscript, beginning with the usual Beta ʾƎsrāʾel eulogy. As there is no visible separation between the two sections and the writing seems to be of the same hand, it must be assumed that a Christian copyist has copied the text, from 33b on, from a Falasha manuscript.

list.item: The volume bears in two places the stamp of an Abyssinian library or monastery, but the text of the stamp is illegible

bibl.title: Draft catalogue of the Ethiopian Manuscripts in the Faitlovitch collection

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
